verify_fix_string: RX-012: find other staff who ARE available
title: Conflict message shows only alt slots for same staff — never mentions other available technicians
failing_behavior: Customer requests Helen at 2pm. Helen is booked. AI says 'Helen is booked at 2pm. Closest available times: 1pm, 3pm.' It never mentions that Tracy or Lisa are free at 2pm. Customer may not realize they can get the same slot with another technician.
root_cause: NailAvailabilityChecker.check() computed altSlots (alternative times for the SAME staff) but never computed altStaff (other staff free at the SAME time). _buildMsg for 'conflict' only used altSlots. No code path produced or surfaced alternative staff names.

conversation.content: I'd like a gel manicure with Helen at 2pm on Monday
Sorry, Helen is already booked at 2 PM. The closest available times with Helen are 1 PM, 3 PM, or 3:30 PM. Which would you prefer?

expected_behavior: AI should also mention that Tracy (and/or Lisa) is available at 2 PM. Example: 'Sorry, Helen is already booked at 2 PM. However, Tracy and Lisa are available at that time. Or I can check Helen at 1 PM or 3 PM. What would you prefer?'
observed_behavior: AI only offers alternative times with Helen. Never suggests switching to a different technician who is free at the customer's preferred time. Customer misses the option entirely.
code_areas: nailsalon/receptionist.js: NailAvailabilityChecker.check() — hasConflict block, must compute altStaff from biz.staff
nailsalon/receptionist.js: _buildMsg() — 'conflict' key, must append staffSuffix with altStaff names
tests/lib/avail-logic.js: checkAvailability() — conflict block, must mirror altStaff computation
notes: altStaff computation reuses the already-loaded `existing` bookings array — no extra Firestore queries needed. The check is O(S) where S = number of staff members. The fix is purely additive: it does not change altSlots behavior, only appends additional information to the conflict message.
